---
title: "Drop test Experiment"
date: today
date-format: long
author: 
  - name: |
      Manuel Cabral <br> Lecturer: Gabriel D. Weymouth
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#00ADEF"
  # Set the white logo as a background image for the title slide
  data-background-image: "imgs/TUDelft_logo_white.png"
  # Position and size the logo as a background element
  data-background-position: "bottom 10px right 30px" # Adjust position
  data-background-size: "170px"  
format:
  revealjs: 
    # embed-resources: true
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: 
      theme: whiteboard
      buttons: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: imgs/TUDelft_logo.png 
    css: styles.css
---

## Drop test: Setup

::: columns

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](imgs/drop_test.png){width=72.5%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](imgs/shapes_volume.png){width=100%}
<br><br>
![](imgs/order_cd.png){width=100%}
:::

:::

## Drop test: Setup

::: columns

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](imgs/drop_test.png){width=72.5%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](imgs/shapes_volume_final.png){width=100%}
:::

:::

## Drop test: Drop time

::: columns

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](imgs/drop_test.png){width=72.5%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%" .v-center}
a. What is the object's terminal velocity?
:::

:::

## Drop test: Terminal velocity

<div style="display: flex; height: 78%; align-items: stretch; gap: 0; font-size: 85%; margin-top: 1em;">

<div style="flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-evenly; padding: 0 2em 0 0.5em;">

<p>At terminal velocity:</p>

$$mg - \rho_w Vg - F_D = 0$$

<p>With quadratic drag:</p>

$$F_D = \tfrac{1}{2}\rho_w C_D A v^2$$

$$\therefore \quad v_t = \sqrt{\dfrac{2(mg - \rho_w Vg)}{\rho_w C_D A}}$$

</div>

<div style="width: 1.5px; background: #ccc; flex-shrink: 0;"></div>

<div style="flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-evenly; padding: 0 0.5em 0 2em;">

<p>Define the mass excess:</p>

$$\Delta m = m - \rho_w V$$

<p>Substituting into $v_t$:</p>

$$\boxed{v_t = \sqrt{\dfrac{2\Delta m\, g}{\rho_w C_D A}}}$$

</div>

</div>


## Drop test: Measuring $C_D$

<div style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-evenly; height: 78%; font-size: 85%; margin-top: 0.5em; padding: 0 3em;">

<p>Holding $\Delta m$, $g$, $\rho_w$, and $A$ constant:</p>

$$v_t = \dfrac{K}{\sqrt{C_D}}, \qquad K = \sqrt{\dfrac{2\,\Delta m\, g}{\rho_w\, A}}$$

<p>With $\Delta m = 1.8\ \text{g}$, $A = \pi(2.5)^2 \approx 19.6\ \text{cm}^2$, $g = 980.7\ \text{cm/s}^2$, $\rho_w = 1.0\ \text{g/cm}^3$:</p>

$$\boxed{K = \sqrt{\dfrac{2 \times 1.8 \times 980.7}{1.0 \times 19.6}} \approx 13.4\ \text{cm/s}}$$

</div>

## Drop test: Fall time & $C_D$

<div style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-evenly; height: 82%; font-size: 80%; margin-top: 0; padding: 0 3em;">

<p>At terminal velocity, the fall time over height $H$ is:</p>

$$T = \frac{H}{v_t} = \frac{H\sqrt{C_D}}{K}$$

<p>Solving for $C_D$ gives $C_D = \left(\dfrac{KT}{H}\right)^2$. With $H = 55.0\ \text{cm}$, $K \approx 13.4\ \text{cm/s}$:</p>

$$\boxed{C_D = \left(\frac{K}{H}\right)^2 T^2 \approx 0.0594\, T^2}$$

</div>

## Drop test: Experiment

<div style="display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; height: 100%;">
  <div style="font-size: 200%; text-align: center;">
    Let's try it!
  </div>
</div>

## Drop test: Experiment Results

$$
\begin{aligned}
T_{sphere} &= \qquad\quad &C_d = \qquad\qquad C_{d,theo} &= 0.47 \\\\
T_{ellipsoid} &= \qquad\quad &C_d = \qquad\qquad C_{d,theo} &= 0.30 \\\\
T_{bullet} &= \qquad\quad &C_d = \qquad\qquad C_{d,theo} &= 0.33\\\\
T_{NACA} &= \qquad\quad &C_d = \qquad\qquad C_{d,theo} &= 0.11
\end{aligned}
$$

## Drop test: Analysis

![](videos/tracker_sphere.webm){width=100%}


## Drop test: Analysis

![](imgs/sphere_parabola.png){width=100%}

## Drop test: Analysis

![](imgs/predicted_vs_experimental_vt.png){width=100%}


## Drop test: Numerical Simulation


<div style="display: flex; 
            align-items: center; 
            justify-content: center; /* <-- Changed to 'center' for minimum horizontal gap */
            width: 100%; 
            margin-top: -250px;       /* <-- INCREASED: Pulls content higher (adjust this value) */
            margin-bottom: 0px;         
            gap: 0px;">                   

  <video src="videos/Sphere.mp4" 
         data-autoplay  
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <video src="videos/Prolate.mp4"
         data-autoplay  
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <img src="imgs/qr_code.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">

  <img src="imgs/tzy_git.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">
  
</div>

## Drop test: Numerical Simulation


<div style="display: flex; 
            align-items: center; 
            justify-content: center; /* <-- Changed to 'center' for minimum horizontal gap */
            width: 100%; 
            margin-top: -250px;       /* <-- INCREASED: Pulls content higher (adjust this value) */
            margin-bottom: 0px;         
            gap: 0px;">                   

  <video src="videos/Sphere.mp4" autoplay loop muted 
         data-autoplay  
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <video src="videos/Bullet.mp4" autoplay loop muted
         data-autoplay   
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <img src="imgs/qr_code.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">

  <img src="imgs/tzy_git.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">
  
</div>


## Drop test: Numerical Simulation


<div style="display: flex; 
            align-items: center; 
            justify-content: center; /* <-- Changed to 'center' for minimum horizontal gap */
            width: 100%; 
            margin-top: -250px;       /* <-- INCREASED: Pulls content higher (adjust this value) */
            margin-bottom: 0px;         
            gap: 0px;">                   

  <video src="videos/Sphere.mp4" autoplay loop muted 
         data-autoplay  
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <video src="videos/Teardrop.mp4" autoplay loop muted
         data-autoplay   
         style="transform: rotate(-90deg); 
                height: 1000px; /* <-- INCREASED: Makes the video physically larger */
                width: auto;
                margin: 0;"></video>
  
  <img src="imgs/qr_code.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">

  <img src="imgs/tzy_git.png" style="width: 500px; height: auto;">
  
</div>


## Thank you!

<div style="display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; height: 100%;">
  <div style="font-size: 150%; text-align: center;">
    For any question, feel free to reach me at  mcabral@tudelft.nl, or at my office, 34 B-2-320.
  </div>
</div>
